assumptions: The dividend receivable and corresponding payable are recognised intragroup monetary items.
The receivable is exposed to foreign currency risk because it is denominated in a currency that creates exchange differences in consolidation.
The related foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation and can affect consolidated profit or loss.
The hedge relationship would otherwise meet the IFRS 9 designation, documentation and effectiveness requirements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup monetary receivable can be analysed under the fair value hedge model because IFRS 9 allows recognised assets or liabilities exposed to a particular risk to be designated as hedged items.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 also provides a cash flow hedge model for variability in cash flows associated with a recognised asset or liability that could affect profit or loss.
